response: "given f(2)=0, we substitute x=2 into 2x³-3x²+ax+b to get 16-12+2a+b=0, which simplifies to 2a+b=-4; for x=2 to be a double root, the derivative f'(x)=6x²-6x+a must also be zero at x=2, yielding 24-12+a=0, so a=-12; substituting a=-12 into 2a+b=-4 gives -24+b=-4, so b=20; thus a=-12 and b=20."
